methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $28M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Heaviside, a Series A–stage Defense & Security company founded by Phillip Walker, has raised $28M to date. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable entities: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. The primary peer set for direct comparison includes three same-sector, same-stage companies: Stark Defence ($500M valuation), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method, anchoring to $28M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $56M. The Peer-Median method examines the three direct comparables, yielding a median valuation of $300M with a range of $150M–$500M. This dispersion reflects variance in stage timing, technology differentiation, or capital efficiency among peers.

Triangulating both methods produces a median valuation of $178M, with a plausible range of $48M (low) to $345M (high). The wide spread reflects methodological divergence: the stage-progression approach anchors conservatively to raised capital, while peer-median reflects the pricing discipline of recent sector financings. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity and runway of peer companies, customer concentration at Heaviside, and the degree to which defense-sector regulatory or procurement headwinds apply uniformly across the cohort.
